zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

purpose: Transfer test (anti-overfit proof) for the dark-factory skill system. See runs/transfer-test-mini/README.md.
created_by: claude-orchestrator at the wealth.com run's PERT bead TB-WC-2026-0508-021

source_signals.reference: RSK-005 in ATTR-WEALTHCOM-001 — factory overfit risk
source_signals.extract: Anti-overfit transfer-test bead is mandatory before wealth.com run closure.

source_signals.reference: df-meta-attractor §guardrails 'do not overfit factory to current example app'
source_signals.extract: Skill system's foundational rule that motivates this run.

attractor_state_rationale: Goal is concrete (apply factory to a deliberately different workload and verify it tailors correctly), scope is narrow (one calc rule + golden tests + tailoring artifacts; no UI), tech stack inherits from wealth.com run (Python 3.12 + pytest), no checkpoints required (this is a sub-run of the parent's already-approved budget).
goal: Demonstrate that the dark-factory skill system applies cleanly to a workload that shares the regulated-fintech archetype but differs in user model, domain entities, document types, and isolation requirements. Specifically: build a TaxBaseline Mini single-user CPA tool with the same TDD + deterministic-calc + ADR + RALPH patterns, and prove no wealth.com vocabulary leaks into it.
scope_in: Meta-attractor + Product Tailoring Profile + Generated Meta-Skill Contract for TaxBaseline Mini
ADR-T-002 'no multi-tenant by design' (documents deliberate divergence from ADR-002)
One calc rule: federal_income_tax_2026 (IRC §1 brackets + standard deduction)
Golden dataset for that rule: ≥10 cases including boundary/negative
Adversarial generic-process-critic review confirming no wealth.com leakage
scope_out: Real 1040/W-2 PDF parsing (out-of-scope; calc accepts structured input)
Other tax rules beyond federal income tax baseline
Any wealth.com-specific noun (Household, Trust, Person, Entity, Asset, GRAT, OBBBA, Sage, etc. — these are not part of the tax baseline domain)

product_specific: TaxBaseline Mini surface and entity model (TaxFiling)
factory_reusable: Deterministic-calc separation pattern (ADR-001 → ADR-T-001 transfer evidence)
Citation-grounded extraction pattern (ADR-003 — applies but no extractor in this minimal scope)
Product Tailoring Profile schema with surface_flags + risk_profile + methodology_blend
meta_meta_governance: Validation that the skill system distinguishes per-archetype tailoring from one-size-fits-all generation
Validation that anti-overfit rules in product-tailoring-profile.json (forbidden wealth.com nouns) are enforceable as a CI check

selected_skills.reason: Authors this record + tailoring + generated meta-skill
RALPH 5-loop on ADR-T-001 + golden test acceptance
Capture lessons that update the wealth.com run's anti-overfit rules

node_contract.what: Build TaxBaseline Mini calc rule + tailoring artifacts + ADRs proving the factory transfers without wealth.com vocabulary leakage.
node_contract.why: Without this evidence, RSK-005 (factory overfit) remains open and the wealth.com run cannot pass final Hawkeye conformance.
node_contract.how: Meta-attractor → tailoring → ADR-T-001/002 → calc rule + golden dataset + tests → adversarial review → final Hawkeye notes anti-overfit pass.
node_contract.when: Now; closes before final Hawkeye on parent run.
node_contract.who: claude-orchestrator (parent delivery owner); adversarial generic-process critic for review.
node_contract.how_good: 100% wealth.com-noun absence in transfer-test-mini/ (excluding parent_run cross-references and explicit comparison tables); ≥10 golden test cases pass; ADR-T-001 demonstrably mirrors ADR-001's structure with different rule content; ADR-T-002 explicitly documents non-transfer of ADR-002 with rationale.

round_1_independent_framing.system_theorist: The transfer test must use a workload that genuinely differs in archetype dimensions, not a thin renaming of wealth.com. Income-tax-for-solo-CPA is well chosen because it lacks multi-tenant, lacks the household-as-aggregate-root, and operates on different IRS code sections. Risk: making the new workload too small to actually exercise the factory. Mitigation: include enough golden test cases to demonstrate the TDD pattern transfers.
round_1_independent_framing.requirements_governance_architect: ADR-T-001 must explicitly trace back to ADR-001 to demonstrate the *pattern* transferred, not the rules. ADR-T-002 must explicitly document the deliberate non-transfer of ADR-002 — a factory that applies multi-tenant isolation to a single-user tool would itself be a slop signal. Lessons feed back to df-feedback-learning.
round_1_independent_framing.verification_safety_critic: Demand a CI-checkable assertion: 'no wealth.com nouns in transfer-test-mini/'. Forbidden tokens: Household, Trust, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, OBBBA, Ester, Sage, Heritage Map, Family Office, EstateFlow, EstateCompass. The test passes if a grep returns zero matches in transfer-test-mini/02_attractor/, /05_artifacts/, and /app/.
round_1_independent_framing.adversarial_generic_process_critic: Hardest test: try to cut-and-paste the wealth.com run's ADR-001 verbatim into this run. If it 'just works', either ADR-001 is too generic OR the new workload is too similar. Real test: ADR-001 had specific clauses about 'estate tax' and 'GRAT 7520 rate' — those must be replaced; only the *separation pattern* should remain. Verifies ADR is workload-specific.

round_2_cross_critique.theorist_to_governance: The forbidden-token list should also include wealth.com's bounded-context names. Add: 'Person', 'Entity', 'beneficiary' (in the trust sense), 'distribution rule'. These are wealth.com-specific even though they sound generic.
round_2_cross_critique.governance_to_theorist: Agreed. But 'audit log' and 'tenant context' are factory-reusable patterns — they shouldn't be on the forbidden list. The line is: forbidden = wealth.com domain vocabulary; allowed = governance/architecture vocabulary that any regulated SaaS would use.
round_2_cross_critique.verification_to_all: The CI grep should also assert the ADR-T-001 explicitly cites ADR-001 as the parent pattern. Anti-overfit doesn't mean 'pretend they're unrelated' — it means 'show the pattern, not the content, transfers'.
round_2_cross_critique.adversarial_to_verification: Add a second adversarial test: read ADR-T-001 and ADR-T-002. If a reader can't tell from those documents alone what the workload is (without the README), the ADRs are too generic and the transfer-test is theatre. Test: someone looking at ADR-T-001 should immediately see 'income tax' and 'IRC §1', not 'tax rules in general'.

round_3_synthesis.decision: PROCEED with TaxBaseline Mini scope. Forbidden-token list locked: 'Household, Trust, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, CLAT, Dynasty, OBBBA, Ester, Sage, Heritage Map, Family Office, EstateFlow, EstateCompass, distribution_rule, beneficiary' (where beneficiary is in the trust-distribution sense; allow 'beneficiary designation' references in tax-form context as that's IRS terminology). ADR-T-001 must cite ADR-001 explicitly as parent pattern. ADR-T-002 must cite ADR-002 as the rule it deliberately does NOT transfer, with rationale.
non_negotiable_constraints_locked_in: ADR-T-* must be workload-specific enough that a reader knows it's tax-baseline-not-estate
CI grep for forbidden tokens runs against transfer-test-mini/ tree

evidence_commitments: ADR-T-001 explicitly cites ADR-001 as the parent transferred pattern + IRC §1 rule content distinguishes it
ADR-T-002 explicitly cites ADR-002 as the deliberately-NOT-transferred rule + rationale
≥10 golden test cases for federal_income_tax_2026 rule pass
Adversarial CI-grep: zero forbidden wealth.com tokens in runs/transfer-test-mini/ (excluding parent_run references in this attractor record)
anti_overfit_checks: ADR-T-001 + ADR-T-002 reader test: workload identifiable from ADRs alone
Generic-process-critic adversarial review confirming no wealth.com vocabulary leaks
next_safe_action: Author product-tailoring-profile.json (different surface flags from wealth.com) and generated-meta-skill-contract.json (different refusal rules from wealth.com — e.g., no 'multi-tenant' rule, but include 'no LLM income-tax-bracket arithmetic' rule). Then ADR-T-001/002. Then calc rule + golden dataset + tests. Then run the CI grep for forbidden tokens.
verdict_rationale: Coherent. Goal narrow. Forbidden-token list locked. Lessons mapped to df-feedback-learning. Evidence commitments are CI-checkable. Proceed.
